chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Before: Justice Muhammad Saleem Jessar Justice Nisar Ahmed Bhanbhro CP No.D-5840 of 2025 (Faizan Hussain v. the Government of Sindh and others) Petitioner: Faizan Hussain through Mr. Mushtaq Ahmed, advocate Respondents: Nemo. Date of hearing and order: 19.01.2026 O R D E R Nisar Ahmed Bhanbhro, J. Through instant petition, the petitioner has claimed the following relief(s): “1. To Direct the Respondents to immediately revive, operationalize, and expand the DLS Mobile Van service across Karachi and Sindh, particularly in Industrial Zones and Educational Institutions. 2. To Direct the Respondents to provide one DLS Mobile Van the Petitioner-organization (Karachi Naujawan Foundation) to facilitate on- the-spot issuance of licences to the public under official supervision. 3. To Order strict action against agent (tout) mafias operating around Licence Centres and ensure a corruption-free environment through proper monitoring. 4. To Direct the Respondents to adopt or integrate the Lahore on-the-spot licensing model in Sindh in order to ensure efficient, dignified, and hassle- free service delivery to the public. 5. Grant any other relief deemed appropriate in the interest.” When confronted as to the entitlement of the petitioner to claim DLS Mobile Van, learned counsel for the petitioner failed to satisfy this Court as to how an NGO can claim for DLS Mobile Van to facilitate the public for issuance of license. Consequently, this petition, being not maintainable, is hereby dismissed in limine along with the pending application(s), with no order as to costs. JUDGE JUDGE HEAD OF CONST.
Consequently, this petition, being not maintainable, is hereby dismissed in limine along with the pending application(s), with no order as to costs. JUDGE JUDGE HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES Nadir *
